claim_limitations: This ledger records design-time MR candidate decisions only. It does not contain SUT execution, empirical pass/fail outcomes, violation rates, or comparative performance claims.

entries.title: Node permutation equivariance
entries.decision_basis: Retained as a representation-level MR because node relabeling should preserve the graph computation when x, pos, y, and face are permuted consistently and outputs are inverse-permuted before comparison.
rubric_reasons: Physical geometry and boundary categories are preserved by reindexing rather than altered.
The follow-up transformation, inverse output mapping, metric, and tolerance are explicitly specified in the MR card.
The current evidence is design-time plus fixture-level asset plumbing, not a real SUT verdict.

evidence_reference.path: theory/MeshGraphNets圆柱绕流MetBench_MR资产编制与验证说明.md

entries.claim_limitations: The retained decision means the MR is structurally specified for future execution. It does not assert that any MeshGraphNets implementation passes or fails the relation.

entries.title: Mirror-y equivariance
entries.decision_basis: Retained as an OOD-stress relation because y-reflection has a clear geometry/vector mapping only under mirror-compatible domains, boundary labels, and velocity-component conventions.
rubric_reasons: The physical basis is conditional on mirror-compatible geometry and boundary labels.
The output mapping is explicit for vector velocity fields: u remains unchanged and v changes sign.
Because these preconditions may move cases outside the nominal training distribution, the relation is retained as OOD stress rather than ordinary design-time evidence.
entries.claim_limitations: The retained OOD-stress decision does not assert that mirror-y is valid for every cylinder-flow encoding and does not report any MeshGraphNets execution result.

entries.title: Discrete divergence boundedness
entries.decision_basis: Deferred because the candidate needs a fixed discrete divergence operator, boundary treatment, and calibrated tolerance before it can support retained MR verdicts.
rubric_reasons: The physical basis is continuity or mass conservation for incompressible flow, not an automatic Noether-derived MR.
The metric and tolerance are not calibrated enough to interpret pass/fail outcomes.
The candidate can remain a diagnostic direction, but the ledger must not turn it into a retained SUT relation yet.

local_markers: Divergence is treated as an incompressible continuity / mass-conservation constraint
No claim may state that Noether's theorem derives the divergence-free condition

entries.claim_limitations: The deferred decision keeps divergence/conservation out of retained pass/fail claims until operator, boundary, and tolerance evidence exist.

entries.title: Viscous time reversal
entries.decision_basis: Rejected as a retained MR for viscous cylinder flow because local physics review records dissipative, non-time-reversal-invariant dynamics in the intended test sense.
rubric_reasons: The physical basis fails for the target viscous cylinder-flow regime.
The local manuscript configuration explicitly excludes time reversal for viscous cylinder flow.
The local v2.1 interrogation identifies time reversal as physically invalid here and uses the exclusion as a validity-rubric example.

local_markers: Time reversal is explicitly excluded for viscous cylinder flow
dynamics are dissipative and not time-reversal invariant

entries.claim_limitations: This rejection is a design-time domain-validity decision. It is not an empirical failure result from a real SUT run.
